title: Reporting a Leak to a Plumbing Company

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Hello, Brightwater Plumbing, Gary speaking. How can I help you today?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: Oh, hello. I've got a leak under my kitchen sink and there's water all over the floor. I really need someone to come out.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Let's get an engineer booked in. First, if you can, turn off the water at the stopcock — it's usually under the sink. Now, could I take your name?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: Yes, it's Helen Ashworth.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: And how do you spell the surname?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: It's A-S-H-W-O-R-T-H. Ashworth.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Thank you. And the address, including the postcode?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: It's eight Meadow Lane — sorry, no, that's my mum's. My address is eight Marlow Lane. Postcode M-twenty, three-R-G.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Not to worry — eight Marlow Lane, postcode M20 3RG. And a phone number where the engineer can reach you?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: Yes, it's oh-seven-seven-hundred, nine-double-oh, one-two-four.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Let me just check — oh-seven-seven-zero-zero, nine-zero-zero, one-two-four. Is that correct?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: That's right.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Good. Now, can you describe exactly where the water's coming from? Is it from the pipe or from the tap itself?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: It's definitely from the pipe — the joint underneath. The tap's fine, it's the pipe that's dripping, quite fast now.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Understood — a leaking pipe joint. I can get someone to you this afternoon. Our engineer, Dave, can be with you between two and four o'clock. Does that work?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: Between two and four is perfect, thank you.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Now, a quick word about charges. There's a call-out fee of forty-five pounds, and that covers the first hour of labour. After that it's thirty pounds per hour, plus any parts. For a leaking joint like this, though, it's usually a quick fix.

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: That's fine, I just want it sorted. Do I pay Dave directly?

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Yes, Dave can take card payment at the door. I'll log this now — your job reference is J, then seven-one-four-two, so J7142. He'll phone you when he's about twenty minutes away.

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: J7142, got it. Is there anything I should do in the meantime, while I'm waiting for Dave?

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: Yes, a couple of things. Keep the water turned off at the stopcock if you can, and pop a bucket or a bowl under the leak to catch the drips. Lay some old towels down too, just to stop the water spreading across the floor. And try not to use the kitchen tap until Dave's had a look.

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: That makes sense. I've already put a bowl under there and it's filling up quite quickly, so I'm glad someone's coming today.

turns.speaker: Gary
turns.text: You did exactly the right thing calling straight away. Leaks like this only get worse if they're left. Dave's very good — he'll have it sorted in no time. And if anything changes before then, just call us back and quote that reference.

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: Wonderful. Thank you so much, Gary.

questionGroups.instructions_html: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questionGroups.prompt: Details of the problem and visit
questionGroups.instructions_html: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.prompt_text: The water is leaking from the ______ (not the tap).
Engineer will arrive between 2 pm and ______.

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: How will Helen pay for the work?

options.text: by card, to the engineer at the door
by bank transfer after the visit
